findings.description: The claim that the in-memory delivery cache reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9% is implausible. If end-to-end latency is sub-second (per Performance section), then uncached latency would be ~1000 seconds — inconsistent with a functioning pipeline.
findings.evidence: "The in-memory delivery cache reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9% compared to unbuffered writes." Combined with: "the pipeline maintains sub-second end-to-end latency for 95th-percentile events." If cached = sub-second, uncached = ~1000 seconds.
findings.suggestion: Replace with a defensible, measured figure and cite the baseline measurement.

findings.description: The claim that 'batch processing is 3× faster than streaming for files under 10MB' is architecturally unusual and presented without methodology. The comparison direction may be inverted.
findings.evidence: "Benchmarks show that batch processing is 3× faster than streaming for files under 10MB. For larger payloads, streaming is preferred." No methodology, environment, or baseline cited.
findings.suggestion: Verify and cite the benchmark methodology. Clarify whether the size threshold and comparison direction are correct.
